scenario: A user asks an AI assistant to turn a poetic idea into a public claim, but the evidence is still early and mostly metaphorical.
signal: A strong idea has appeared, but claim-risk is present.
invitation: Develop the idea as a philosophy and systems-design scaffold.
consent: The user wants development, but public readers need clear boundaries.
alignment: Keep beauty and accountability together. Separate metaphor from proof.
action: Create public-safe docs, app, schemas, and receipt builder.
outcome: The idea becomes usable without overclaiming.
renewal: Review language, add falsifiers, and adjust if readers misunderstand.
evidence: User-provided concept, master spec, governance gates, claim boundary.
assumptions: A local-first app helps make the model practical.
unknowns: Which communities will find the first use case most helpful.
